This kernel appears to be a synthetic/handcrafted microbenchmark rather than something from a known benchmark suite or tutorial. The specific characteristics — the arbitrary prime-number strides (17, 257), the power-of-two masking (`& 4095`), the fixed 8-iteration loop with a multiply-add accumulation using `1.1f` — look like someone constructed this to test specific memory access patterns or arithmetic throughput behavior. It doesn't match any well-known benchmark (e.g., Rodinia, Parboil, SHOC) or standard CUDA tutorial code that I'm aware of.
